normalized_title: Position Statement: Container Deposit Scheme Infrastructure
scope_summary: WAPC position statement on how Container Deposit Scheme (CDS) infrastructure (container collection cages, in-shop/bag-drop return points, reverse vending machines, container deposit recycling centres and large-scale facilities) should be considered and assessed in the WA planning system. It applies across Western Australia to all CDS infrastructure, sets assessment considerations (amenity, parking, access, signage, safety) and includes a model local planning policy (Appendix 1) with development standards under which specified CDS development is exempt from development approval pursuant to Schedule 2, Part 7, clause 61 of the LPS Regulations.

key_numeric_standards.topic: separation of recycling centre from residential
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: distance from the building in which a container deposit recycling centre operates to the boundary of any lot used for residential purposes (incl. mixed use)
large reverse vending machine and/or container collection cage from an adjoining lot boundary accommodating a residential use

key_numeric_standards.topic: setback from road reserve / right-of-way intersection or crossover
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: large reverse vending machine and/or container collection cage (sightline preservation)

key_numeric_standards.topic: container collection cage maximum footprint and height
large reverse vending machine maximum footprint and height (not on car parking land)
key_numeric_standards.value: 45 m2 footprint; 3 m height; max dimensions 8 m x 6 m
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: large reverse vending machine proposed outdoors on land not used for car parking

key_numeric_standards.topic: large reverse vending machine maximum area within car park
key_numeric_standards.value: greater of 4 car parking spaces, or 45 m2 (car park <=200 spaces) / 75 m2 (car park >=200 spaces)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: large reverse vending machine within an existing car park of more than 40 spaces

key_numeric_standards.value: 1 cage per lot; 1 large RVM per 15,000 m2 total lot area (non-car-park land); 1 large RVM per 1,000 car parking spaces (car parks >40 spaces)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: outdoor container collection cages and large reverse vending machines
large reverse vending machine adjacent to land accommodating a residential use, absent approved opening hours of the predominant use

key_numeric_standards.topic: waste bin provision for large RVM over 10 m2 footprint
key_numeric_standards.value: 1 waste bin and 0.5 recycling bins (240L) per 10 m2 of development footprint
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: outdoor large reverse vending machine exceeding 10 m2 development footprint
CDS infrastructure erected on a temporary basis (exempt under deemed provisions cl 61(1)(f) and (2)(d))

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
AS/NZS 1158.3.1:2005 Lighting for roads and public spaces, Part 3.1

quality_flags: multi-column PDF extraction interleaves columns, scrambling sentence order in body sections (Appendix 1 standards extracted cleanly)
summary.json title is garbled ('Department of Planning,') and key_provision headings are scrambled
minor OCR/typo artifacts in source text ('environmentt', 'approvall', 'required for t of some forms')
pages 9-11 are diagrams (large reverse vending machine, recycling centre, car queuing) with minimal extractable text - expected
